title: Touwswil Flea Market draws a happy crowd
description: GEORGE NEWS - Visitors to the Touwswil Flea...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - Visitors to the Touwswil Flea Market on Saturday 7 September had an enjoyable time with a variety of local stalls offering from plants and pottery to delicious homemade fare to munch on, live music, dancing and creative kids' activities.


> The market is non-profit and is a collaborative initiative between the communities of Hoekwil and Touwsranten with the aim of bringing the two communities together.


Another goal of this initiative is to create economic opportunities for entrepreneurs in Touwsranten.

From left: Khaya Timothy Gould and Kari Smit from Let's Plant Nursery Hoekwil with Thomas Duane Hodgson of Kentia Palms Hoekwil.

Mandie and Roeleen van Raaij of Poppy Hill Gardens show what a mother and daughter can create: proudly South African jaffles and lots of other yummy treats.

Touwswil Flea Market is a market for the community by the community.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
